notes: Forcing Steam Play with new beta update. Window that allows choice between Direct X version appears. Upon choosing either option, game doesn't even load, steam just gets stuck at running. Tweaks don't work. Trying to skip the game launcher by renaming game files doesn't work.
title: Saints Row: The Third

notes:
SR3 runs great after I disabled esync.   There are a few minor glitches, such as Cyrus Temple not speaking in one cutscene, but other than that, extremely playable.  Before I disabled esync, the game was still playable, but would pause for about half a second every 10 or so seconds.   Gets extremely frustrating.   

Disabled esync and that went away.
title: Saints Row: The Third

notes: Game has Linux port, but it's shitty port actually. You will have less fps then in wine. Anyway Proton refuses to launch this game, neither tweaks works. Tryed all versions of proton, with no luck. Launcher works just fine, but game doesn't launch. Process appears but window not creating.
title: Saints Row: The Third

notes:
The Native version of the Game runs. Thought it not possible to play the Game on Lan if the person you want to play with own the game. Also sadly the game doesn't run as well on Linux than on Windows. But it is possible to play on Lan if one person plays an Windows and the other on Linux. This has to be improved.
But if you want to play solo, then it will be fine to play on Linux
title: Saints Row: The Third

cpu: AMD FX-7500 Radeon R7, 10 Compute Cores 4C+6G
notes: Native version doesn't work very well, stucks for half a second everytime I turn mouse too fast, Proton version only runs to launcher and doesn't run game either on DX11 and DX9, even with all suggested tweaks applied.
title: Saints Row: The Third

cpu: AMD A10-5800K APU with Radeon HD Graphics
notes: Ran great with and without Esync.  I ran without it cause it seemed to freeze up less with it.  Screen would freeze up every once in a while for up to 3 seconds, but overall it was very playable.
title: Saints Row: The Third
